## HMM v3 Special Section

Track whether geometry features improved false vol-expansion avoidance and mid-vol chop detection.

## Model Usefulness Summary

- `heuristic` T+1: exact accuracy is lower than adjacent-tolerant accuracy, so most misses are neighboring-regime errors.
- `heuristic` T+1: false-alarm rate is elevated (46.5%); model may over-call expansion risk.
- `heuristic` T+1: Brier VIX Spike=0.4574, combined vol-direction accuracy=58.2%.
- `heuristic` T+2: exact accuracy is lower than adjacent-tolerant accuracy, so most misses are neighboring-regime errors.
- `heuristic` T+2: false-alarm rate is elevated (43.6%); model may over-call expansion risk.
- `heuristic` T+2: Brier VIX Spike=0.4894, combined vol-direction accuracy=54.4%.
- `heuristic` T+3: exact accuracy is lower than adjacent-tolerant accuracy, so most misses are neighboring-regime errors.
- `heuristic` T+3: false-alarm rate is elevated (43.0%); model may over-call expansion risk.
- `heuristic` T+3: Brier VIX Spike=0.5206, combined vol-direction accuracy=52.7%.

- `hmm_v1_core` T+1: missed-risk rate is elevated (29.1%); risk transitions may be under-warned.

### Plain-English Interpretation

- HMM v3 may be too conservative and may under-warn before higher-vol transitions.
